content.problemStatement:
Chef will have NNN guests in his house today. He wants to serve at least one dish to each of the NNN guests. Chef can make two types of dishes. He needs one fruit and one vegetable to make the first type of dish and one vegetable and one fish to make the second type of dish. Now Chef has AAA fruits, BBB vegetables, and CCC fishes in his house. Can he prepare at least NNN dishes in total?


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains of a single line of input, four integers N,A,B,CN, A, B, CN,A,B,C.


Output Format
For each test case, print "YES" if Chef can prepare at least NNN dishes, otherwise print "NO". Print the output without quotes.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains four space‑separated integers N, A, B, C — the number of guests, the number of fruits, vegetables and fishes respectively.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if Chef can prepare at least N dishes, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: Use 1 fruit+1 veg and 1 veg+1 fish → 2 dishes. Test case 2: Only 2 veg, both used for fruit dishes, no veg left for fish dishes → 2 dishes < 3. Test case 3: min(6,2+3)=5 dishes, which is ≥4. Test case 4: min(3,1+1)=2 dishes < 3.

solution.approach: Each dish consumes exactly one vegetable, therefore the total number of dishes cannot exceed B. It also consumes either one fruit or one fish, so the total cannot exceed A + C. The maximum possible dishes is thus min(B, A + C). If N is less than or equal to this value, Chef can serve all guests; otherwise he cannot.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) — constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — only a few integer variables.
